Premiere Columbus 2025 Delivers Education, Inspiration, and New Experiences for Midwest Beauty Pros
data.description:
COLUMBUS, OH / ACCESS Newswire / November 13, 2025 / Premiere Show Group, the nation's leading network of beauty industry events and professional education, successfully concluded Premiere Columbus 2025, energizing the Midwest beauty community with two days of world-class education, high-energy competition, and innovative attendee experiences. Held September 14-15 at the Greater Columbus Convention Center, the show brought together 10,000 visits of licensed professionals and students alongside 129 exhibiting brands for a weekend of skill-building, discovery, and networking.

Education That Inspires

Attendees explored more than 148 educational sessions led by 80+ top educators and influencers. From hands-on workshops to business development classes, Premiere Columbus gave beauty professionals the tools to advance their careers and expand their craft. Main Stage presentations from Wayne Tuggle, Candy Shaw, Kell Grace, Danny Amorim, and Kirsty Meakin energized audiences, while CE-certified courses from Monique Waters of Wu Aesthetics Academy and Jaime Schrabeck of Precision Nails allowed licensed professionals to meet continuing education requirements.

"I truly think Premiere Columbus is a major event for our city," said Zhana, a cosmetology student from Ohio. "You get to discover so many new products, meet brands, attend classes, connect with people - everything is super interesting. I felt like I learned so much, yet at the same time, I didn't get to see everything. It was such a great experience!"

Competitions Showcase Skill and Creativity

Adding to the show's high-energy atmosphere, the Ultimate Barber Battle powered by L3VEL3 and sponsored by JRL took over the Main Stage on Monday. Barbers from across the region competed in a live showdown celebrating creativity, precision, and technical mastery-drawing enthusiastic crowds and spotlighting the next generation of barbering talent. Antoine Lewis Hardie earned first place, with every competitor gaining visibility and high-value tools to enhance their craft.

A Dynamic Exhibit Floor

The exhibit hall featured 129 professional beauty brands, offering product demos, hands-on learning, and event-only promotions. Attendees engaged directly with brands including Chi by Farouk, Wahl, JRL, Malibu C, Johnny B, Kell Grace, Duomo Pro, Boulevard, Glownar Aesthetics, Hale Cosmeceuticals, Procell, Fierce Hair Extensions, Rude Cosmetics, LeChat, Izutech, and Martini Beauty, discovering new products and solutions for their businesses.

New Experiences Bring Energy and Connection

Premiere Columbus enhanced the attendee experience with several fresh activations. Each morning, DJ KICKIT welcomed attendees with high-energy beats in the Atrium Lobby. Interactive photo ops across the show floor and lobby encouraged professionals to capture and share the weekend's excitement.

Attendees also visited The Branded Salon booth for exclusive Premiere Columbus swag and custom merchandise, while Monday morning's Sample Bag Giveaway treated the first 250 attendees to product-filled bags from top brands-including Duomo Pro, Chi by Farouk, Oli G, Izutech, Rude, Jack Winn Pro, Hale Cosmeceuticals, Modologie Face Systems, TOWELHUB/The Shears Depot, LeChat, PureO Natural Products, and Martini Beauty-claimed within minutes and generating major buzz.

Looking Ahead: Premiere Shows 2026

The excitement doesn't stop-Premiere Shows are already planning another year packed with education, inspiration, and unforgettable experiences. Beauty professionals won't want to miss a single event in the 2026 lineup:

  • Premiere Anaheim | February 1-2, 2026

    Registration is Now Open! Click HERE to register.

  • Premiere Orlando | May 30-June 1, 2026

  • Premiere San Antonio | August 30-31, 2026

  • Premiere Columbus | September 28-29, 2026

Each show will continue to deliver cutting-edge education, exclusive hands-on experiences, and unparalleled opportunities to connect with the top brands and brightest minds in the industry. Whether discovering the latest products, learning new techniques, or building connections that last a lifetime, Premiere Shows 2026 will be the ultimate destination for beauty professionals ready to elevate their careers.

Visit premiereshows.com and follow @premierebeautyshows for updates and announcements.

###

Rachel Brill

rachel@premiereshows.com

The Premiere Shows are the nation's leading network of beauty industry events, matched with world-class education from top beauty brands and renowned educators. Premiere welcomes all representatives of the professional beauty industry to meet with top brands from around the globe, get hands-on education, shop, connect with their community, and stay up to date with the hottest trends in the business. Premiere exhibitors are guaranteed to meet decision-makers from every area of the beauty industry on an exhibit floor designed to bring the beauty community together to learn, buy, and connect. For more information on Premiere Shows and its events, visit premiereshows.com.

The Premiere Shows' events are organized by USA Beauty LLC, a joint venture between Informa Markets, BolognaFiere, and the Professional Beauty Association - a historic partnership created to better serve the beauty industry. USA Beauty LLC also operates the Cosmoprof North America events held annually in Las Vegas and Miami, and Beauty New York - a new consumer-focused event launching in October 2025.

SOURCE: Informa Markets - USA Beauty

View the original press release on ACCESS Newswire

data.title: Brazil cuts forecasts for 2025 economic growth, inflation
data.description:
Brazil's Finance Ministry on Thursday trimmed its 2025 economic growth forecast to 2.2% from 2.3%, citing weaker gross domestic product (GDP) growth estimated in the third quarter that also affected its outlook for the final quarter.

"Indicators suggest that activity continued to slow in the third quarter," the ministry said. "This slowdown was already expected, reflecting the lagged and cumulative effects of the restrictive monetary policy currently in place."

Brazil's benchmark interest rate is at a near two decade-high of 15% as the central bank seeks to bring inflation back to its 3% target, which has a tolerance band of 1.5 percentage points on either side.

The ministry's economic policy secretariat cut its 2025 inflation projection to 4.6% from 4.8%, still above that goal.

data.title: Best Gold IRA Companies 2026 Ranked (Top Rated Gold IRA Rollover Providers Announced)
data.description:
Based on industry trends, consumer insights and market research, IRAEmpire has prepared its updated list of the Best Gold IRA Companies in the USA.

NEW YORK CITY, NEW YORK / ACCESS Newswire / November 13, 2025 / As inflation, rising national debt, and stock market volatility continue to challenge traditional retirement portfolios, many Americans are turning to Gold IRAs as a way to preserve their wealth and hedge against uncertainty. A Gold IRA, unlike a standard retirement account, allows investors to hold physical gold and other precious metals that historically maintain value even during economic downturns.

According to Ryan Paulson, Chief Editor at IRAEmpire, "Over the past few years, Gold IRAs have gained significant traction among retirees, pre-retirees, and long-term investors who want more control over their savings. However, the growing popularity of this asset class has also led to a surge in new providers - each claiming to offer the best service, lowest fees, and highest security."

Overview of Best Gold IRA:

  • Augusta Precious Metals (Best for Large Investments)

  • Lear Capital (Best Customer Support)

  • Goldencrest Metals (Best Prices)

  • Preserve Gold (Best Products)

Choosing the right company is crucial. Fees, storage safety, transparency, and customer support can vary dramatically between providers. That's why this guide evaluates and ranks the Best Gold IRA Companies in 2025 based on reputation, trust, and overall value to investors.

After an in-depth review, Augusta Precious Metals ranks #1 overall, followed by Lear Capital, Goldencrest Metals, and Preserve Gold - each excelling in specific areas such as education, service, pricing, or diversification.

This article will help you understand what sets these firms apart, how Gold IRAs work, and what factors to consider before opening an account.

Find the Best Gold IRA Company in Your State Here.

What Is a Gold IRA and How It Works

A Gold IRA is a type of self-directed individual retirement account (IRA) that allows investors to hold physical precious metals - such as gold, silver, platinum, or palladium - instead of traditional assets like stocks and bonds. These accounts operate under the same IRS rules as conventional IRAs, but they're managed through a specialized custodian who facilitates the purchase, storage, and reporting of physical assets.

How It Works

When you open a Gold IRA, your funds are used to purchase IRS-approved bullion or coins that meet specific purity standards (e.g., 99.5% for gold). The metals are then stored in a secure, third-party depository approved by the IRS. These facilities - such as the Delaware Depository or Brinks Global Services - provide insurance coverage and meet strict security requirements.

Gold IRAs can be established as:

  • Traditional Gold IRAs - Contributions may be tax-deductible, and earnings grow tax-deferred until withdrawals.

  • Roth Gold IRAs - Contributions are made with after-tax dollars, but qualified withdrawals in retirement are tax-free.

Funding a Gold IRA

Most investors fund their Gold IRA by transferring or rolling over existing retirement accounts, such as:

  • Traditional IRA

  • Roth IRA

  • 401(k) or 403(b) from a previous employer

These rollovers can typically be completed without triggering taxes or penalties when done correctly through trustee-to-trustee transfer, meaning your funds move directly from one custodian to another.

Why Investors Choose Gold IRAs

Gold has historically served as a hedge against inflation, currency depreciation, and market volatility. By holding a tangible asset within a tax-advantaged retirement account, investors can diversify their portfolio while maintaining long-term stability.

In short, a Gold IRA offers the dual benefits of physical asset ownership and retirement tax advantages - making it an appealing option for those looking to protect and preserve wealth over decades.

Read the Full Best Gold IRA Companies Rankings Here.

How IRAEmpire Ranked the Best Gold IRA Companies

With dozens of firms offering similar services, identifying the best Gold IRA companies requires careful analysis beyond marketing claims. To ensure this ranking remains fair and informative, we evaluated each provider using verifiable criteria that reflect what matters most to retirement investors in the U.S.

1. Reputation and Trustworthiness

A strong reputation is the foundation of any reliable Gold IRA company. We examined:

  • Ratings and reviews from trusted platforms like Better Business Bureau (BBB), Business Consumer Alliance (BCA), and Trustpilot.

  • Company history and years in business.

  • Transparency in addressing customer complaints or disputes.

Established providers with consistent positive feedback and clear business practices scored higher.

2. Fee Transparency and Costs

Gold IRAs often include several layers of fees - setup, maintenance, storage, and dealer markups. Companies that clearly disclose these costs and avoid hidden charges ranked better. We also gave preference to providers offering flat-rate fee structures, as they tend to be more predictable for long-term investors.

3. Buyback and Liquidity Options

The ability to sell or liquidate metals easily is a key part of retirement planning. Companies offering guaranteed buyback programs or no-fee liquidation earned additional points for investor convenience and peace of mind.

4. Education and Customer Support

Because Gold IRAs can be complex, companies that focus on educating clients - through free webinars, personalized sessions, or transparent learning resources - ranked higher. We also considered customer service responsiveness, accessibility, and post-sale support.

5. Security and Storage Options

We reviewed each provider's depository partners, insurance coverage, and security standards. Firms using IRS-approved vaults like Delaware Depository, Brinks, or IDS of Texas were rated positively for compliance and safety.

6. Overall Value to the Investor

Finally, we considered the total customer experience - combining trust, cost, transparency, and long-term reliability. Companies that deliver consistent performance across all metrics earned higher rankings in our 2025 list.

Augusta Precious Metals - Ranked #1 and Best Overall

Founded in 2012 and headquartered in Beverly Hills, California, Augusta Precious Metals has become one of the most trusted names in the U.S. Gold IRA industry. Known for its educational approach and commitment to transparency, Augusta stands out by helping investors understand every aspect of the Gold IRA process before committing funds. The company's mission centers on empowering retirement savers through knowledge, not pressure-based sales tactics.

Read Augusta Precious Metals Reviews, Fees and Services Analysis.

IRAEmpire's Findings on Augusta Precious Metals Reviews:

Augusta holds an A+ rating from the Better Business Bureau (BBB) and the highest AAA rating from the Business Consumer Alliance (BCA). It also maintains excellent customer satisfaction across independent platforms - a testament to its client-first philosophy. Prominent financial educators and analysts have highlighted Augusta for its clarity in explaining fees, policies, and rollover procedures.

Fee Structure and Transparency

One of Augusta's defining traits is its fully transparent fee model. The company clearly discloses all costs upfront, including one-time account setup and annual storage or maintenance fees. There are no hidden markups or surprise charges, allowing investors to make decisions with confidence.

Storage and Security

Clients can choose from IRS-approved depositories such as Delaware Depository or Brinks Global Services, both of which offer high-level physical security and full insurance coverage. Augusta's partnership with these vaults ensures that investors' metals remain safely stored and fully compliant with IRS regulations.

Education and Support

Augusta places a strong emphasis on investor education. New clients can attend one-on-one web conferences explaining the risks and benefits of precious metal investing, how rollovers work, and what to expect during account setup. This educational-first approach makes Augusta especially appealing to retirees and cautious investors.

Ideal For

Augusta Precious Metals is best suited for:

  • Retirees or pre-retirees looking for long-term wealth preservation.

  • High-net-worth individuals who prioritize security and transparency.

  • First-time Gold IRA investors who value personal guidance and clarity.

In summary, Augusta's consistent reputation, educational outreach, and upfront pricing make it the #1 Gold IRA company of 2025 for transparency, reliability, and investor trust.

Lear Capital (Ranked #2 Among The Best Gold IRA Companies)

Founded in 1997, Lear Capital is one of the longest-standing precious metals dealers in the United States, with more than two decades of experience serving retirement investors. The company is well-known for its reliability, broad product selection, and consistent customer service. Lear's focus on client education and accessibility makes it a strong contender for investors who prefer an established brand with a proven track record in both physical gold and IRA-backed assets.

Read Lear Capital Reviews, Fees and Services Analysis.

Findings on Lear Capital Reviews

Lear Capital maintains a solid reputation supported by an A+ rating with the Better Business Bureau (BBB) and high customer satisfaction scores across multiple platforms. The company has handled billions in precious metal transactions, earning trust through its transparent practices and responsive client support.

Fees and Transparency

Lear Capital's pricing is competitive and clearly disclosed during the account setup process. Its IRA setup fee and annual storage costs are straightforward, and investors receive full disclosure before committing funds. Unlike some smaller firms, Lear provides detailed documentation outlining all costs, minimizing confusion for new investors.

Product Range and Flexibility

One of Lear Capital's major advantages is its wide selection of IRA-eligible coins and bullion, including gold, silver, platinum, and palladium. This variety allows investors to diversify beyond just gold and tailor their portfolio to market trends or personal risk tolerance.

Customer Service and Education

Lear is recognized for its responsive support team and investor resources. Its website features educational guides, market insights, and regular updates on precious metal trends. Account representatives are available to walk clients through rollover steps and ensure that transactions remain compliant with IRS rules.

Ideal For

Lear Capital is best suited for:

  • Investors seeking a long-established provider with strong industry experience.

  • Retirement savers who value broad metal selection and flexible account options.

  • Individuals who prioritize responsive communication and ongoing service.

In summary, Lear Capital ranks #2 in 2025 for its longevity, wide product range, and reliable customer service - a strong choice for investors who prefer an experienced, client-oriented Gold IRA provider.

Goldencrest Metals (Ranked #3 Among The Best Gold IRA Companies)

Founded in 2020, Goldencrest Metals is a newer entrant in the Gold IRA market but has quickly gained attention for its competitive pricing and personalized service. While it lacks the decades-long history of larger firms, Goldencrest compensates through transparency, affordability, and investor-focused support - attributes that have helped it build credibility among first-time Gold IRA investors.

Read Goldencrest Metals Reviews, Fees and Services Analysis.

Reputation and Growth

Despite being relatively young, Goldencrest Metals maintains strong ratings on independent review platforms and has earned positive feedback for its no-pressure approach. The company emphasizes long-term client relationships rather than quick sales, an increasingly important factor for retirement investors seeking reliability in newer firms.

Fees and Pricing Advantage

Goldencrest stands out for offering some of the lowest markups in the industry. It uses a simplified, transparent pricing model that appeals to cost-conscious investors. The company also provides upfront disclosure of any setup or storage fees before account activation - avoiding the hidden costs that often deter new investors.

Storage and Security

Goldencrest partners with trusted, IRS-approved storage providers such as Brinks Global Services and International Depository Services (IDS). These facilities provide full insurance coverage, robust physical security, and regular audits to ensure asset safety and compliance.

Customer Experience and Education

Clients frequently note the accessibility and responsiveness of Goldencrest's account representatives. The company provides educational materials, free consultations, and rollover guidance for investors transitioning from traditional IRAs or 401(k)s. Its personalized approach makes it appealing for investors who value one-on-one support over large-scale operations.

Ideal For

Goldencrest Metals is best suited for:

  • New investors entering the Gold IRA space who want low entry costs.

  • Cost-conscious savers seeking competitive pricing without compromising safety.

  • Individuals preferring hands-on, personalized service rather than large corporate structures.

In short, Goldencrest Metals ranks #3 in 2025 for bringing affordability, clarity, and human touch to an industry often dominated by long-established giants - making it a standout choice for investors prioritizing cost efficiency and transparency.

Preserve Gold (Ranked #4 Among The Best Gold IRA Companies)

Preserve Gold, founded in 2021, has positioned itself as a trusted choice for investors seeking diversification across multiple precious metals. While many firms focus primarily on gold, Preserve Gold offers a balanced range of gold, silver, platinum, and palladium IRA options - giving clients more flexibility in how they protect their retirement savings. Despite being newer than some competitors, the company has quickly earned credibility through its transparent policies and strong customer engagement.

Read Preserve Gold Reviews, Fees and Services Analysis

Findings on Preserve Gold Reviews

Preserve Gold has received favorable reviews for its educational resources and consistent communication. The company holds positive ratings on platforms like BBB and Trustpilot, reflecting investor satisfaction with its straightforward approach and accessible support. While it doesn't yet have the long-term track record of established firms, its growing client base signals strong early trust.

Diversified Product Offerings

Unlike many single-metal IRA firms, Preserve Gold allows investors to build multi-metal portfolios, enabling broader hedging strategies. This flexibility helps investors balance exposure across metals that may perform differently under various economic conditions - for example, pairing gold's stability with silver's industrial demand potential.

Fees and Storage Security

Preserve Gold maintains a clear and competitive fee structure, with all costs disclosed upfront. Investors can choose from IRS-approved storage partners such as Delaware Depository or Brinks Global Services, both offering full insurance and secure auditing standards. This ensures compliance and safety for all IRA-held assets.

Customer Education and Experience

Preserve Gold provides a user-friendly onboarding process and educational guides designed to simplify the Gold IRA setup. Their specialists walk clients through account creation, metal selection, and rollover options from existing IRAs or 401(k)s - making it accessible for first-time investors.

Ideal For

Preserve Gold is best suited for:

  • Investors who want diversification across gold, silver, platinum, and palladium.

  • Retirement savers who value personalized guidance and straightforward pricing.

  • New investors looking for clarity and support throughout the IRA setup process.

In summary, Preserve Gold ranks #4 among the best Gold IRA companies in 2025 for its focus on diversification, transparent pricing, and investor education - making it a compelling choice for those who want more than just gold in their retirement portfolios.

What to Consider Before Opening a Gold IRA

Opening a Gold IRA can be a smart move for those seeking long-term stability, but it's important to understand the key factors that affect performance, compliance, and overall costs. The right company can simplify the process, while the wrong one may expose you to unnecessary fees or risks.

1. Minimum Investment Requirements

Most Gold IRA companies require a minimum investment ranging from $10,000 to $50,000. Augusta Precious Metals, for example, caters to higher-net-worth investors with a $50,000 minimum, while others like Goldencrest and Preserve Gold make entry easier for beginners. Understanding this threshold helps align your investment with your financial goals and liquidity.

2. Fees and Maintenance Costs

Gold IRAs come with setup fees, annual administrative fees, and storage charges. Some firms use flat-rate pricing, while others use percentage-based fees. Transparent, upfront disclosure is key - always ask for a full breakdown before signing.

3. Storage and Custodian Options

By law, all physical metals in a Gold IRA must be stored with an IRS-approved custodian or depository. Look for companies that partner with trusted vaults like Delaware Depository or Brinks Global Services, offering full insurance and segregation options for your metals.

4. Buyback Policies

Liquidity matters - particularly in retirement planning. The best companies offer no-fee buyback programs, allowing you to sell your metals quickly and fairly at market value. Firms like Augusta and Lear are known for transparent buyback policies that simplify liquidation.

5. Educational Support and Guidance

Gold IRAs can be complex, especially for new investors. Prioritize companies that emphasize education and informed decision-making through webinars, guides, and personal consultations.

Find the Best Gold IRA Provider of Your State on IRAEmpire

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

1. Are Gold IRAs tax-deductible?

Gold IRAs follow the same tax rules as traditional and Roth IRAs.

  • Traditional Gold IRA: Contributions may be tax-deductible, and earnings grow tax-deferred until you make withdrawals during retirement.

  • Roth Gold IRA: Contributions are made with after-tax dollars, meaning withdrawals in retirement are tax-free, provided you meet the IRS's qualifying conditions.

However, tax advantages depend on your income level and filing status, so it's best to consult a tax professional before investing.

2. Can I store my gold at home?

No. The IRS requires that all metals in a Gold IRA be stored in an IRS-approved depository under the supervision of a qualified custodian. Storing your IRA gold at home would count as a distribution, potentially leading to taxes and early withdrawal penalties.

Ryan says, "If you wish to hold gold personally, consider purchasing it separately outside your IRA account."

3. How safe is a Gold IRA?

Gold IRAs are considered relatively stable, as gold tends to retain value even when paper assets fluctuate. However, like all investments, they carry risks - including metal price volatility and custodian reliability. Choosing a reputable provider with insured, audited storage facilities greatly enhances safety and compliance.

4. What happens when I sell or withdraw from my Gold IRA?

When you retire or reach age 59½, you can:

  • Sell your metals through your IRA custodian and receive cash proceeds.

  • Take physical possession of your metals as an in-kind distribution (taxable for traditional IRAs).

Top companies simplify this process through structured buyback programs that offer competitive market rates.

5. How do I fund a Gold IRA?

You can fund a Gold IRA through:

  • Direct transfers from an existing IRA.

  • Rollovers from a 401(k), 403(b), or similar employer plan.

    When done via trustee-to-trustee transfer, these movements are non-taxable and penalty-free.

6. Is now a good time to invest in a Gold IRA?

While timing depends on individual goals, many U.S. investors turn to gold during periods of inflation, geopolitical tension, or market uncertainty. Because gold often moves inversely to stocks, adding it to a retirement portfolio can help balance risk and preserve value over time.

Read the Full List of the Best Gold IRA Companies in the USA.

About IRAEmpire

IRAEmpire is a leading online resource dedicated to helping investors make smarter decisions about retirement planning, precious metals, and alternative investments. Built with a mission to simplify complex financial topics, IRAEmpire provides in-depth reviews, comparison guides, and educational content focused on Gold IRAs, Silver IRAs, and other self-directed retirement accounts.

The platform is designed for investors who want clarity, transparency, and unbiased insights before committing to a retirement strategy. Unlike generic finance sites, IRAEmpire specializes in the precious metals niche, ensuring that its articles, rankings, and company reviews are tailored specifically to the needs of retirement savers seeking stability in uncertain times.

Key features include detailed breakdowns of top Gold IRA companies, step-by-step investment guides, and market trend analysis. By combining data-driven research with easy-to-understand explanations, IRAEmpire empowers readers to compare providers, evaluate fees, and choose the right partner for their long-term financial security.

Whether you're a first-time investor exploring precious metals or an experienced retiree looking to diversify, IRAEmpire serves as a trusted guide. Its goal is simple: to help you protect and grow your wealth through smart, informed retirement planning.

CONTACT:

Ryan Paulson

ryan@iraempire.com

data.title: Inside information: HONKARAKENNE INITIATES CHANGE NEGOTIATIONS AS PART OF THE COMPANY’S PROFITABILITY PROGRAM
data.description:
HONKARAKENNE OYJ Stock Exchange Release Inside information 13 November 2025 at 19:00

Inside information: HONKARAKENNE INITIATES CHANGE NEGOTIATIONS AS PART OF THE COMPANY’S PROFITABILITY PROGRAM

Honkarakenne Oyj initiates change negotiations in accordance with the Finnish Act on Co-operation within Undertakings. The negotiations are based on financial and production-related grounds as well as the need to reorganize operations. The primary objective is to enhance the company’s profitability and competitiveness by streamlining processes and clarifying the organizational structure.

As part of the planned restructuring, Honkarakenne is also evaluating potential technology partnerships to improve operational efficiency and strengthen its ability to meet future development challenges.

The negotiations will involve all personnel, excluding members of the Executive Group and employer representatives participating in the process.

The negotiations will begin on 19 November 2025 and are expected to conclude during December. Outcomes may include redundancies, temporary layoffs, part-time employment, or changes to essential terms of employment.

The estimated total reduction need corresponds to 19 person-years. Any decisions resulting from the negotiations are expected to be implemented by 31 May 2026 at the latest.

The ongoing profitability program aims to achieve savings of at least EUR 2.5 million, of which personnel-related costs are estimated to account for approximately EUR 1.3 million.

If implemented, the restructuring plans may result in adjustment items being recorded in the income statement. The exact amount of any such items will be determined following the conclusion of the change negotiations.

If implemented, the plans may result in adjustment items being recorded in the income statement. Any potential adjustment items will be specified after the end of the change negotiations.

HONKARAKENNE OYJ

Marko Saarelainen

President and CEO

Further information:

Marko Saarelainen, President and CEO, tel. +358 40 542 0254, marko.saarelainen@honka.com or

Maarit Jylhä, CFO, tel. +358 40 594 4099, Maarit.jylha@honka.com

DISTRIBUTION

Nasdaq Helsinki Ltd

Key media

Financial Supervisory Authority

www.honka.com

Honkarakenne Oyj manufactures high-quality, healthy and ecological log homes, holiday homes and public buildings under its Honka® brand from Finnish solid wood. The company has delivered 90,000 buildings to over 50 countries. House kits are manufactured in Finland, the company's own factory is located in Karstula. In 2024, Honkarakenne Group's net sales were EUR 36.7 million, of which exports accounted for 29 %. www.honka.com

data.title: KGHM's profit rises on Sierra Gorda strength, higher prices
data.description:
Polish state-run copper producer KGHM on Thursday reported an 80% year-on-year rise in third-quarter net profit, driven by the strong performance of its Chilean Sierra Gorda mine and higher precious metal prices.

WHY IT'S IMPORTANT

KGHM is one of the world's largest copper and silver producers. Its earnings are highly sensitive to global commodity prices, making it a barometer for global industrial demand.

CONTEXT

The Sierra Gorda mine in Chile, a joint venture with Australian miner South32, has become one of the main engines of KGHM's profit growth.

The mine's adjusted core profit for the first nine months surged 60% year-on-year to $970 million, fuelled by higher production volumes of copper and molybdenum, and higher metal prices, the company said.

OUTLOOK

Looking ahead, KGHM's cost base is expected to fall after the Polish government announced in May that it would cut the country's copper mining tax, which is paid solely by the company, from 2026, in a move expected to boost the miner's future profitability.

BY THE NUMBERS

The company posted third quarter net profit of 433 million zlotys ($119.07 million), missing a 663 million zlotys forecast seen in a Reuters poll. Its revenue was at 8.32 billion zlotys, compared to a forecast of 8.46 billion zlotys.

($1 = 3.6365 zlotys)

data.title: Trump foes Comey and James challenge appointment of US attorney
data.description:
Key points:
  • Bondi's appointment of Halligan faces scrutiny
  • Judge demands full records of grand jury presentation
  • Other Bondi appointments were deemed unlawful
  • Charges against Comey and James could be invalidated

By Sarah N. Lynch and Andrew Goudsward

Lawyers for former FBI Director James Comey and New York Attorney General Letitia James asked a federal judge on Thursday to drop the criminal charges against them, arguing that President Donald Trump's hand-picked U.S. attorney who obtained the indictments against them was unlawfully appointed.

The hearing at the federal courthouse in Alexandria, Virginia, marked the first time a judge considered one of several efforts that James and Comey - two prominent critics of Trump who each oversaw investigations into him - have made to dismiss the indictments before trials.

U.S. District Judge Cameron McGowan Currie did not issue a decision during the hour-long court hearing. Currie said she would issue her ruling later this month.

The arguments center on whether Lindsey Halligan, Trump's former personal attorney, was illegally installed as interim U.S. attorney for the Eastern District of Virginia. If Halligan is deemed to be unlawfully appointed, then the charges against Comey and James could be invalidated since Halligan was the only federal prosecutor to present evidence to the grand juries in both matters.

"The only thing that matters is whether Ms. Halligan had a proper appointment when she stood before the grand jury, and she did not," said Ephraim McDowell, one of Comey's attorneys.

Comey has pleaded not guilty to charges of making false statements and obstructing Congress, while James has pleaded not guilty to charges of bank fraud and lying to a financial institution.

Both were charged by Halligan's office shortly after Trump openly called on Attorney General Pam Bondi to prosecute them.

Bondi appointed Halligan at Trump's request in September, after her predecessor, Erik Siebert, was forced out of the job after expressing concerns about a lack of evidence to support criminal charges against both Comey and James.

APPOINTMENT LENGTH LIMIT

Attorneys for Comey and James argue that Halligan's appointment violates a federal law they said limits the appointment of an interim U.S. attorney to one 120-day stint. Repeated interim appointments would bypass the Senate confirmation process and allow a prosecutor to serve indefinitely, they said.

Siebert had been previously appointed by Bondi for 120 days and was then appointed by the U.S. District Court for the Eastern District of Virginia, since the Senate had not yet confirmed him in the role.

Currie, a South Carolina-based federal judge appointed by former Democratic President Bill Clinton, was assigned to decide the issue given the role federal judges in Virginia played in Siebert's appointment.

The Justice Department has argued that Halligan's appointment was lawful, saying that nothing in the law "explicitly or implicitly precludes the Attorney General from making additional appointments."

In an effort to bolster its case, Bondi also belatedly, in late October, separately gave Halligan a second title of "Special Attorney" and said she is authorized to supervise both prosecutions.

Outside legal experts, however, have said the Justice Department's unusual maneuvers to install Halligan could derail the cases.

Three federal judges in other cases have already separately ruled against the Justice Department on the issue, finding that Bondi unlawfully appointed U.S. attorneys in New Jersey, Nevada and Los Angeles.

In addition, a Justice Department memo penned in 1986 by Samuel Alito, who is now one of the Supreme Court's conservative justices, interprets the law the same way Comey and James do.

data.title: Mattr Corp. Down Over 21%, On Pace for Largest Percent Decrease Since June 2020 — Data Talk
data.description:
Mattr Corp. (MATR.T) is currently at C$8.13, down C$2.17 or 21.07%

  • Would be lowest close since Sept. 27, 2022, when it closed at C$8.00
  • On pace for largest percent decrease since June 11, 2020, when it fell 31.88%
  • Currently down two consecutive days; down 21.68% over this period
  • Worst two day stretch since the two days ending June 11, 2020, when it fell 37.94%
  • Down 25.69% month-to-date
  • Down 35.68% year-to-date; on pace for worst year since 2020, when it fell 71.91%
  • Down 86.49% from its all-time closing high of C$60.16 on July 2, 2014
  • Down 42.83% from 52 weeks ago (Nov. 14, 2024), when it closed at C$14.22
  • Down 42.83% from its 52-week closing high of C$14.22 on Nov. 14, 2024
  • Would be a new 52-week closing low
  • Traded as low as C$8.00; lowest intraday level since Sept. 28, 2022, when it hit C$8.00
  • Down 22.33% at today's intraday low; largest intraday percent decrease since June 11, 2020, when it fell as much as 33.16%

data.title: DAX Snaps 3-Day Rally
data.description:
Germany's DAX weakened to close about 1.4% lower at 24,043 on Thursday, on profit-booking after a three-day rally, with investors focused on the official end of the US shutdown which granted funding until January.

However, uncertainty around the release of impendent major economic releases kept markets cautious.

At the same time, traders assessed a mixed batch of corporate earnings.

Siemens plunged over 9%, leading the day’s losses, after reporting a drop in fourth-quarter earnings despite raising its medium-term sales growth forecast. This prompted declines in other companies within the conglomerate, including Siemens Energy (-5.4%) and Siemens Healthineers (-3.4%).

On the upside, Merck gained roughly 5% boosted by stronger-than-expected third-quarter results.

data.title: RCS - Schroder Inc Growth - Results analysis from Kepler Trust Intelligence
data.description:
RNS Number : 4929H Schroder Income Growth Fund PLC 13 November 2025  

Schroder Income Growth (SCF)

13/11/2025

Results analysis from Kepler Trust Intelligence

Schroder Income Growth (SCF) has reported NAV and share price total returns of 9.6% and 12.9% respectively for the year to 31/08/2025, against the FTSE All-Share's 12.6%. Performance was supported by an overweight to financials, where banks and insurers delivered higher dividends and buybacks, lifting share prices. However, above-market exposure to small- and mid-caps and not holding a few strong performers, such as Rolls-Royce and British American Tobacco, weighed on relative returns.

During the financial year, the board took several steps to bolster shareholder returns, including a reduction in the management fee from 0.45% to 0.40%, effective from 01/09/2025, now charged on the lower of market capitalisation or NAV, removal of a secretarial and admin fee, and tighter discount management.

Kepler View

Schroder Income Growth (SCF) delivered another year of dividend progress, with its total dividend rising 3.5%, supported by a 7.8% increase in earnings. This marks 30 consecutive years of dividend growth, with dividends rising at an annualised 4.1%, comfortably ahead of CPI inflation at 2.5%, delivering real dividend growth of 1.6% per annum since launch. Although earnings did not fully cover the dividend, we view positively the board's decision to make all distributable reserves available, providing flexibility to support future payouts and underscoring its commitment to maintaining SCF's strong record of dividend growth. The board has also delivered on its plan to smooth the income, rebalancing payments more evenly across all four dividends so shareholders receive a larger proportion of the total dividend earlier in the year.

Across the market, unfavourable exchange rates and a shift toward share buybacks have been headwinds to UK dividends. Against that backdrop, SCF's growth in both earnings and dividend is encouraging, underpinned by its broad income sources, exposure to businesses buying back shares sustainably and the board's supportive reserve policy. The trust's 4.4% yield stands above the UK market, the Bank of England base rate, and the AIC UK Equity Income sector average. And as interest rates decline, we think this premium yield, combined with a long-term dividend growth record, should become increasingly appealing versus cash or bond alternatives, whilst SCF also offers investors the potential for capital growth.

Whilst a tilt toward smaller companies can increase short-term volatility, it has historically enhanced long-term returns and contributed to SCF's three-decade dividend growth record. Given current mid-cap valuations, their dividend growth and earnings recovery potential, SCF's overweight exposure offers meaningful upside if sentiment improves, whilst also supporting the portfolio's underlying income potential.

At the time of writing, SCF trades at an 8.5% discount, within its five-year average. We think the narrowing over the year reflects several potential drivers: ongoing buyback activity, a premium yield appealing in a falling rate environment, a long-standing dividend growth record and lower management fees. Should performance also improve, notably by re-ratings in the trust's small- and mid-cap holdings, there is potential for the discount to tighten further.

Investors should, however, recognise that uncertainties remain. The upcoming Budget, geopolitical tensions and ongoing tariff discussions could continue to weigh on sentiment and pressure smaller, more domestically oriented companies, potentially affecting earnings, margins and valuations in the near term. Overall, though, we believe SCF offers a well-balanced proposition for investors seeking both income and long-term growth, backed by its proven dividend record and access to Schroders' deep research resources. We think the trust's yield, valuation discipline and mid-cap bias could prove rewarding for patient investors if sentiment towards the UK market improves.

CLICK HERE TO READ THE FULL REPORT 

Visit Kepler Trust Intelligence for more high quality independent investment trust research.

data.title: Cognizant to Acquire 3Cloud, Creating a Leading Force in Microsoft Azure Services and Enterprise AI Transformation
Cognizant To Acquire 3Cloud, Creating A Leading Force In Microsoft Azure Services And Enterprise AI Transformation >CTSH
Cisco Stock Is Headed for a 25-Year High. AI Networking Gear Is in High Demand. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Angela Palumbo

Cisco Systems stock was rising Thursday after fiscal first-quarter earnings showed that artificial-intelligence demand is helping the networking company grow.

Cisco reported better-than-expected fiscal first-quarter earnings and revenue after the stock market closed on Wednesday, while also providing strong guidance for the second quarter and fiscal year.

Networking revenue of $7.8 billion beat analyst estimates of $7.5 billion and was a standout figure in the quarter. Cisco's networking segment includes gear used in AI data centers, and it has seen growth as enterprise demand for AI infrastructure continues to rise.

"Networking has an AI play in optics and silicon — as AI orders at hyperscalers accelerated to $1.3B," Melius Research analyst Ben Reitzes wrote in a research note on Thursday. He raised his price target on Cisco to $100 from $84 while maintaining a Buy rating on the stock.

"We think this networking momentum can continue as this space has a clear secular tailwind," he added.

UBS analyst David Vogt also raised his price target to $90 from $88 on Thursday while maintaining a Buy rating on the stock, "following the combination of solid F1Q results led by strong 'Product' orders, a F2Q guide better than feared, and a bump to the FY26 revenue and EPS outlook."

As of midday Thursday, Cisco shares were up 4.3% and on pace for their highest close since March 31, 2000, according to Dow Jones Market Data. The stock, which was the best performer in the Dow Jones Industrial Average on Thursday, is now down only 3.6% from its all-time closing high of $80.06, hit on March 27, 2000.

The stock has now risen 30% this year, outperforming the 16% gain of the S&P 500. Still, of the 25 analysts surveyed by FactSet, 14 rate the stock as a Buy while 10 say it's a Hold and one says it's a Sell.

"Execution remains crisp with strong AI orders, good FCF [free cash flow], cost controls, margin strength (even in light of higher input costs) and buy-backs," Raymond James analyst Simon Leopold wrote on Wednesday night. Leopold rates the stock as Market perform without a price target, though, as he believes Cisco looks "fairly valued at this time."

Shares of Cisco are trading at 18.3 times earnings expected over the next 12 months, which is above the 5-year average of 14.4 times forward earnings.

Write to Angela Palumbo at angela.palumbo@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: WeightWatchers to offer Novo Nordisk's Wegovy pill after 2026 launch
data.description:
By Bhanvi Satija

WeightWatchers' CEO said on Wednesday it planned to offer Novo Nordisk's much-anticipated Wegovy in pill form if it is launched next year in the United States, deepening its tie-up with the Danish obesity drugmaker.

The collaboration is a potential boost for WeightWatchers as it emerges from bankruptcy and highlights its commitment to offer branded obesity drugs, unlike rivals that push cheaper copies in a highly competitive market.

"We have been working with Novo Nordisk ahead of time to support the launch of oral weight-loss medication," WeightWatchers CEO Tara Comonte said in an interview.

"A lot of people don't want an injection. And the convenience of a pill is going to be huge," she said.

Weight loss pills from Novo and rival Eli Lilly are under consideration for a new, speedy U.S. review pathway that can shave months from the normal approval process.

"If and when it is approved, we will communicate the ways in which we will make it accessible and affordable in line with our mission to serve patients in the U.S. with obesity," a spokesperson for Novo Nordisk said.

The Danish drugmaker expects a decision during the fourth quarter.

WeightWatchers could keep more customers on its platform than telehealth rivals, as doctors expect U.S. patients to switch to the branded medicines once prices come down under a deal announced last week by the White House.

Lilly and Novo agreed to slash prices of their popular GLP-1 injectable drugs for U.S. government health programs and cash-paying customers.

Starter doses of the weight-loss pills, if approved, will cost $149 a month for Medicare and Medicaid enrollees and for cash payers through the U.S. administration's new TrumpRx site.

"Anything that brings pricing down for these medications is good for WeightWatchers," Comonte said.

WeightWatchers is also trying to gain a stronger foothold in women's health, including through tailored programs that offer GLP-1 medicines and hormone replacement therapy.

Outside of the U.S., Germany and the UK are major markets for WeightWatchers, which had 4 million subscribers worldwide at the end of 2023.

data.title: FTSE MIB Extends Gains Near Four-Year High
data.description:
The FTSE MIB closed flat 44,834 on Thursday, remaining near its highest level since January 2021 as markets continued to assess corporate earnings while the resumption of activity for the US government supported the outlook for growth.

Assicurazioni Generali gained 1.7% after reporting double-digit profit growth for the first nine months, while Enel advanced 0.5% ahead of its Q3 earnings release.

In contrast, Terna fell 0.9% after posting weaker-than-expected results.

Azimut Holdings plunged nearly 10% after the Bank of Italy flagged serious governance and organizational flaws at its subsidiary, deeming it unfit for major operations.

Poste Italiane slipped 1% despite strong quarterly results.

data.title: ICE Canola Slightly Higher, Comparable Markets Mixed
data.description:
WINNIPEG, Manitoba--ICE canola futures were slightly higher amid mixed sentiment from comparable oils.

Chicago soyoil was down on Thursday, while European rapeseed was up and Malaysian palm oil was mixed. Crude oil made small gains, correcting from Wednesday's losses.

An analyst said despite recent optimism that Canada and China will eventually resolve their trade war, the timeline is unknown.

About 36,200 canola contracts traded at 11:25 a.m. EST.

   Mar       658.90 up 0.60
